title: Task card (dispatcher → agent)
description: The card the Layer-0 bus/dispatcher produces (from a Matrix message, a tracker transition, or a system condition) and delivers to an agent — the unit of 'you have something to look at'. LOCKED interrupt model: only three classes may interrupt Janet mid-task — a direct Parker/Eli command, a safety issue, or a clarification on the ACTIVE task. Everything else queues (interrupt_policy=defer) and reaches the agent in the compact inbox digest. The dispatcher stamps sender_class server-side; interrupt_policy=principal_command is only honored when sender_class=principal, and task_clarification only when task_id matches the recipient's currently-leased task. LOCKED tracker tie: every card carries the tracker task id it belongs to (spawn-from-task).

card_id.description: Unique card id; consumers de-duplicate on it (delivery may be retried over the backchannel).

task_id.description: Tracker task this card belongs to. Required: the tracker is the source of truth and every agent action ties to a task id — a card about brand-new work is created AFTER the dispatcher files the task.

sender.description: Originating identity: a Matrix user id (e.g. '@parker:petalnet…') or a canonical agent handle.

sender_class.description: Stamped by the dispatcher from its own roster, never trusted from the sender. principal = Parker/Eli; agent = a fleet member; system = dispatcher/watchdog/monitor origin.

recipient.description: Canonical lowercase handle of the agent this card is addressed to.

priority.description: 0=P0 (highest) .. 3=P3 — same scale and direction as the tracker's tasks.priority.

thread.description: Conversation thread this card belongs to (Matrix event id of the thread root, or a bus-assigned thread id). Replies MUST carry the same thread so conversations stay joined across transports.

requires_reply.description: true = the sender expects an answer card back on the same thread; the dispatcher tracks it as unanswered until one arrives.

interrupt_policy.description: defer = queue for the inbox digest, never interrupts (the default for everything). The ONLY three interrupting classes: principal_command (direct Parker/Eli command; honored only when sender_class=principal), safety (a safety condition), task_clarification (clarification on the recipient's ACTIVE task; honored only when task_id matches its current lease).

body.description: The instruction / message content, verbatim (forward, don't paraphrase).

capability.description: Capability tag the work needs (lane gate); the dispatcher only routes a card to an agent whose registry capabilities include it.

lease.description: Present when the card accompanies/announces a claimed lease on task_id (viewer-safe projection — the claim_token travels only in the direct claim response to the worker, never on a card).

expires_at.description: Optional card TTL — after this the dispatcher may drop an undelivered card (e.g. a stale status ping).
